interpretation_framework.form_name: Next Play - Fundamentals of AI Productivity Pre-Event Survey

high_signal.name: What AI tools or features are you currently using? (select all that apply)
high_signal.rationale: Reveals current AI maturity and toolset diversity. Multi-select indicates depth of AI adoption.

high_signal.name: What's your biggest challenge with AI tools right now?
high_signal.rationale: Primary pain point identification - directly informs session content priorities.

high_signal.name: How do you FEEL about AI right now?
high_signal.rationale: Emotional sentiment affects learning receptivity. Scale 1-5 enables sentiment scoring.

high_signal.name: What would make this session valuable for you?
high_signal.rationale: Direct expectation setting - matches content to participant needs.

high_signal.name: How much time per week do you estimate you spend on repetitive computer tasks?
high_signal.rationale: Quantifies opportunity size for AI productivity gains.

demographic.name: What's your current role or title?
What function or department do you work in?
How would you describe your current career stage?

open_ended.name: What are 1-3 repetitive, computer-based tasks that drain your energy and reduce your productivity?
open_ended.rationale: High-signal qualitative data - identifies specific automation opportunities.

screening.name: Are you a member of the Next Play community AND attending the Jan 29 Fundamentals of AI Productivity?
screening.rationale: Eligibility gate - 'No' responses should be excluded from analysis.

analysis_hypotheses: Participants who self-report as 'advanced users' will have higher 'time spent on repetitive tasks' values (inverse relationship - advanced users optimize earlier)
Sentiment score (Q5) will positively correlate with 'current AI usage' level (Q4)
Participants reporting 'overwhelmed by options' as their challenge will select multiple tool types in Q2 (paradox of choice)
Those seeking 'templates/prompts to copy' (Q6) will skew toward lower 'current AI usage' levels
B2B AI and Agentic AI tool users will report higher sentiment scores compared to those only using consumer chatbots
Role/function will correlate with tool preferences (e.g., engineers may prefer IDE/ADEs, marketers prefer Canva/Jasper)

expected_correlations.source_name: How would you describe your current AI usage?
expected_correlations.target_name: How do you FEEL about AI right now?
expected_correlations.rationale: Higher usage typically correlates with higher comfort/excitement

expected_correlations.source_name: What's your biggest challenge with AI tools right now?
expected_correlations.target_name: What would make this session valuable for you?
expected_correlations.rationale: Challenge type should map to desired value (e.g., 'don't know where to start' → 'simple tactics')

expected_correlations.source_name: What AI tools or features are you currently using?
expected_correlations.target_name: How would you describe your current AI usage?
expected_correlations.rationale: More diverse tool selection should correlate with higher usage self-assessment

expected_correlations.source_name: How do you FEEL about AI right now?
expected_correlations.target_name: How much time per week do you estimate you spend on repetitive computer tasks?
expected_correlations.rationale: Higher AI optimism may correlate with lower perceived wasted time (already optimized)

themes_to_watch.description: Participants using many AI tools but feeling overwhelmed - indicates quantity over quality adoption
Advanced users stuck at plateau level - indicates need for intermediate/advanced content
Revealing how much time respondents report on repetitive tasks - potential AI productivity gain
Movement from consumer chatbots to B2B/agentic tools as maturity indicator
Mismatch between what attendees want (templates vs. mindset) vs. what challenge suggests they need

ai_maturity_score.description: Composite score based on tool diversity (bxyM), usage frequency (sLMq), and sentiment (qurD)

productivity_opportunity_hours.description: Average hours per week on repetitive tasks, weighted by number of respondents
productivity_opportunity_hours.calculation: mean of moGb responses mapped to hourly values

tool_adoption_heatmap.description: Cross-tabulation of role/function vs. tool types

exclusions.responses_to_exclude: Responses where dGZw value is 'No' (not attending)
exclusions.fields_to_exclude_from_correlations_reas: Demographic fields and open-ended text excluded from statistical correlations - used for segmentation and qualitative analysis only

form_summary.form_name: Next Play - Fundamentals of AI Productivity Pre-Event Survey

handoff_notes: Framework created based on form structure. 4 submissions currently available. Key insight: This is a pre-event survey for an AI productivity workshop, so analysis should focus on informing session content. Sentiment question (qurD) uses emoji labels but values are stored as text - D1.2 should handle emoji-to-score mapping. The open-ended 'repetitive tasks' question (nkxv) is high-value for identifying use cases but may have null responses (as seen in sample). Recommend D1.3 challenge the 'adoption paradox' hypothesis - it's possible high tool diversity genuinely reflects experimentation rather than poor focus.
